LAW AND MOTION 302, LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT W. WONG CONSTRUCTION CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT AFFIRMED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE EXCEPTION THAT THE COURT CORRECTS THE LANGUAGE WHERE IT REFERS TO THE "THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION" TO READ AS THE "FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION." THEREFORE, THE FOLLOWING WAS ADOPTED: DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES BOTH AN "IMPROPER SLOPE" AND "BUILDING FLOOR BELOW THE INTERIOR LOBBY ENTRANCE." WONG'S MOTION ONLY ADDRESSES THE "IMPROPER SLOPE." FURTHER, WONG FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE DOORS WERE THE EXCLUSIVE CAUSE OF THE FLOODING. THE COURT ALSO OVERRULED THE OBJECTIONS TO MR. NORMAN'S DECLARATION. NORMAN DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT W. WONG CONSTRUCTION CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Mattaniah Eytan, Esq. of Law Offices of Mattaniah Eytan 399-1000 for x-deft & moving party; Steven S. Miyake, Esq. 421-4400 for x-complainants & opposing parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court affirmed its tentative ruling with the exception that the Court corrects the language where it refers to the "third cause of action" to read as the "fourth cause of action." Therefore, the following was adopted: DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES BOTH AN "IMPROPER SLOPE" AND "BUILDING FLOOR BELOW THE INTERIOR LOBBY ENTRANCE." MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JAIDIN CONSULTING GROUP, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED ANSWER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.
